key_drivers: Falling real yields (DFII10 at 1.76%) supporting gold, tech, and risk assets
US-Iran geopolitical conflict elevating VIX to 23.57, driving safe-haven demand for gold and JPY
Massive crude inventory build (+15.989M barrels) and oversupply pressuring CL despite geopolitical support
BoJ hawkish tightening cycle and Fed expected cuts driving JPY strength (6J bullish)
Risk-off environment and China PMI contraction (49.0) creating dual headwinds for AUD (6A bearish)
RBA hawkish hike to 3.85% partially offset by China slowdown and global risk-off sentiment

data_quality.notes: All primary sources verified. FRED data 1 business day stale but within acceptable range per protocol. VIX confirmed at 23.57 via CBOE. DXY at 98.931 via TradingView. MOVE at 77.75 via TradingView. SOX at 7,764.88 via TradingView. GDPNow Q1 2026 at 3.0% via Atlanta Fed. EIA crude build +15.989M barrels (week ending Feb 20). Gold ETF inflows $3.72B last week. China NBS PMI 49.0 (Feb). Eurozone PMI 50.8 (Feb). RBA hiked to 3.85% on Feb 3. BoJ at 0.75%, hawkish. ECB at 2.00%, dovish hold. Fed 97.5% hold probability at March FOMC.
